title: moments.jsonl (HuggingFace release — the runnable benchmark set)
description: One frozen key moment per line: a human-annotated tutoring moment cut at its resolved cut point, packaged self-contained for replay — pre-cut transcript context, gold dimension (scaffolding vs rigor), the real human continuation for the oracle synthetic student, and provenance back to the source transcripts. 520 moments: 260 scaffolding / 260 rigor. This is the set `tutorsim run` consumes; it is built once by maintainers (tutorsim-build) and never reconstructed at runtime.
$comment: The companion moments.manifest.json carries integrity hashes: content_hash (SHA-256 over the canonical JSON of the records as normalized by the runtime loader — comparable across the Hugging Face and local load paths) and file_sha256 (this file's bytes). provenance.cut_votes is a list of {cut_turn, votes} pairs rather than a map because Arrow (the Hugging Face datasets backend) cannot represent variable-key maps faithfully; the runtime normalizes it back to a map on load.

id.description: `{set_name}:{conv_id}__hum_{turn_start}_{turn_end}`. Matches `scenario_id` in benchmark_520_full_run.jsonl (without the set_name prefix).

context.description: The transcript prefix the tutor model sees: every turn up to and including the cut point, with the REAL (non-sequential) turn numbers from the source transcript.

dimension.description: The human annotators' aggregate judgment of what this moment calls for. Always equals rubric.gold.

mode.description: The synthetic-student mode this set was built for: the oracle student replies in the voice of the real student, guided by the reference continuation.

reference.description: The real human continuation after the cut point ('Turn N. ROLE: text' lines). Empty for the rare moment whose session ended at the cut.

trait.description: The frozen student persona the paper's simulated student ran with, recovered from the paper-run trait cache. Generated from the PRE-cut transcript prefix only (no leakage of the reference continuation). Embedding it makes the benchmark reproducible against the same students: re-generated traits are a different student population, so score differences could not be attributed to the tutor model alone.

persona.description: Trait persona text injected into the student simulator's system prompt.

generator_model.description: Model that generated the persona. The paper-run set is mixed: 510 scenarios from claude-opus-4-6, 10 from a later claude-opus-4-8 top-up.

generated_at.description: UTC timestamp the persona was generated (paper-run cache provenance).

gold.description: Gold label the replay is scored against. Always equals dimension.

hint.description: The representative annotator's situation text (why this moment calls for scaffolding/rigor).

turn_number.description: Real turn number from the source transcript (non-sequential where enrichments were interleaved).

conv_id.description: `{tutor_id}_{student_id}_{transcript_id}` (all UUIDv5 surrogates). The trailing UUID joins to transcripts.transcript_id and ground_truth.conversation_id.

cut_turn.description: Final cut point after role adjustment (a TUTOR-role modal cut decrements by 1 so the AI replaces the human tutor turn). context ends here; the replay starts at cut_turn + 1.

turn_start.description: Start of the annotated moment span.

turn_end.description: End of the annotated moment span.

moment_id.description: The representative annotation's moment UUID (joins to ground_truth.key_moments[].moment_id). Null for moments annotated on the v1 interface, which had no moment ids.

chosen_cut_turn.description: The modal cut vote before role adjustment (ties break to the smallest).

cut_votes.description: Annotators' cut-turn votes within the span, as {cut_turn, votes} pairs sorted by cut_turn. A list (not a map) so the record round-trips through Arrow unchanged; the runtime loader normalizes it back to a map.

cluster_size.description: Number of annotator moments in the (conv, turn_start, turn_end) cluster this moment was resolved from.
